1.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)
figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
1.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

1.gl: graciousness (as gratifying), of manner or act (abstract or concrete; literal, figurative or spiritual; especially the divine influence upon the heart, and its reflection in the life; including gratitude)
1.kj: acceptable, benefit, favour, gift, grace(- ious), joy, liberality, pleasure, thank(-s, -worthy)

1.gl: peace (literally or figuratively); by implication, prosperity
1.kj: one, peace, quietness, rest, + set at one again
1.dv: probably from a primary verb (to join);

1.gl: a calling out, i.e. (concretely) a popular meeting, especially a religious congregation (Jewish synagogue, or Christian community of members on earth or saints in heaven or both)
1.dv: from a compound of G1537 (ἐκ) and a derivative of G2564 (καλέω);

2.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
2.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

3.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)
figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
3.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

3.gl: persuasion, i.e. credence; moral conviction (of religious truth, or the truthfulness of God or a religious teacher), especially reliance upon Christ for salvation; abstractly, constancy in such profession; by extension,
love, i.e. affection or benevolence; specially (plural) a love-feast

3.dv: from a primary (to anticipate, usually with pleasure);

4.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
4.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

5.gl: sacred (physically, pure, morally blameless or religious, ceremonially, consecrated)
a good message, i.e. the gospel

something said (including the thought); by implication, a topic (subject of discourse), also reasoning (the mental faculty) or motive; by extension, a computation; specially, (with the article in John) the Divine Expres
5.kj: account, cause, communication, X concerning, doctrine, fame, X have to do, intent, matter, mouth, preaching, question, reason, + reckon, remove, say(-ing), shew, X speaker, speech,

sacred (physically, pure, morally blameless or religious, ceremonially, consecrated)

9.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
9.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

10.gl: the sky; by extension, heaven (as the abode of God); by implication, happiness, power, eternity; specially, the Gospel (Christianity)
